title: Nettle infusion for hay fever
subtitle: A traditional antihistamine infusion for seasonal allergic rhinitis
excerpt: A hot infusion of dried nettle leaf, taken daily during hay fever season. Nettle has a long traditional history as an antihistamine herb and a small body of clinical evidence supporting its use for mild allergic rhinitis. One of the most cost-effective self-care measures for spring and summer hay fever.
sourceNotes: Maud Grieve, A Modern Herbal (1931), nettle entry, anti-allergic and tonic properties. Mittman P. (1990), 'Randomized, double-blind study of freeze-dried Urtica dioica in the treatment of allergic rhinitis', small pilot trial showing 58% of participants rated nettle moderately effective for hay fever. EMA HMPC assessment of Urtica dioica (2011), traditional use for supportive treatment of joint and urinary conditions; note that the hay fever application is traditional rather than EMA-confirmed at this time.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Dried nettle leaf keeps 12 months in a sealed tin. Brew each cup fresh.

glossaryTerms.definition: The medical term for hay fever, an allergic inflammatory response in the nasal passages triggered by pollen, dust mites, or other airborne allergens.

content.text: A daily cup of nettle leaf tea during hay fever season. Nettle is the herbal tradition's response to

, Maud Grieve notes anti-allergic properties; a small pilot trial by Mittman (1990) found that 58% of participants rated freeze-dried nettle moderately effective for hay fever relief. The mechanism is not fully understood, but nettle contains compounds that inhibit histamine formation and mast cell degranulation, which are central to the allergic response. Nettle is not a replacement for antihistamine medication in severe hay fever, but it is a reasonable daily supportive measure for mild symptoms and is rich in iron, calcium, and silica, a useful nutritive tonic alongside its antihistamine action.

attrs.title: Important safety notes
attrs.body:
Not medical advice. Consult a qualified herbalist or doctor for ongoing or serious symptoms.

Nettle leaf at infusion strength is well tolerated and has no significant drug interactions at normal tea doses. Dried nettle does not sting, the formic acid that causes the sting is deactivated by drying.

Blood thinners: nettle contains vitamin K, which supports blood clotting. Very large regular amounts (multiple litres of nettle tea daily) could theoretically affect INR in people on warfarin, at one to three cups a day, this is not a clinical concern for most people, but inform your prescriber if you are on warfarin and begin taking nettle regularly.

See your doctor for severe hay fever that is interfering significantly with daily life, work, or sleep, there are highly effective prescription and OTC treatments available. See your doctor for hay fever accompanied by asthma symptoms, severe itchy eyes, or anaphylaxis after allergen exposure.

items.prepNote: about 1 heaped teaspoon of dried leaf
optional, nettle tea is mild-tasting, grassy, and unsweet

content.text: Place {{dried-nettle-leaf}} of dried nettle leaf in the teapot or infuser. Pour {{water}} of just-off-boil water over and put the lid on immediately. Steep for 10 minutes. Strain and drink. The infusion is mild-tasting, grassy and green, somewhere between spinach water and a light herb tea. Honey balances the mild bitterness if needed.

One to two cups daily throughout hay fever season, from the first signs of pollen until the season ends. The traditional approach is to begin one to two weeks before your typical hay fever start date and continue consistently throughout the season, rather than waiting until symptoms are already established. For mild hay fever, this preventive approach may reduce peak symptom severity. For moderate-to-severe hay fever, use alongside conventional antihistamines rather than instead of them.
When NOT to use this remedy

See your doctor for severe hay fever significantly affecting work, sleep, or quality of life. Do not use nettle infusion as the sole management for hay fever with asthma symptoms, asthmatic reactions to allergens need prompt medical care. Discuss regular nettle tea use with your prescriber if you are on warfarin.
